subject: Sports
title: GPS Tracking in Team Sports
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on GPS Tracking in Team Sports.

hook.text: That small pod between a footballer's shoulder blades tracks every sprint, turn, and heartbeat in real time.

buildup.text: GPS vests collect data on distance covered, top speed, acceleration, and workload during training and matches.

discovery.text: A Premier League midfielder covers about 10-13 km per match. GPS reveals the exact intensity of every minute.

twist.text: Coaches use GPS data to prevent injuries. If a player's workload spikes too fast, they're rested before breakdown.

climax.text: GPS transformed coaching from intuition to data science. Every decision now has numbers behind it.

punchline.text: A chip the size of a coin tells coaches more than their eyes ever could.

quiz.question: How do coaches use GPS tracking data?
options: To monitor player workload and prevent injuries
To track fan attendance at games
To measure the weight of players
